name: SNETsocket
doc:
Function FASTCALL SNETsocket(stype As UByte) As Byte

Create a Spectranet socket.

Parameters:
    stype: socket type (1=STREAM, 2=DATAGRAM, 3=RAW)

Returns:
    Socket ID (Byte), or negative on error.

name: SNETbind
parameters: socket As UByte, port As UInteger
doc:
Function FASTCALL SNETbind(socket As UByte, port As UInteger) As Byte

Bind a socket to a local port.

Parameters:
    socket: socket ID returned by SNETsocket
    port: local port number

name: SNETlisten
doc:
Function FASTCALL SNETlisten(socket As UByte) As Byte

Put a socket into listening state for incoming connections.

Parameters:
    socket: socket ID returned by SNETsocket

name: SNETaccept
doc:
Function FASTCALL SNETaccept(socket As UByte) As Byte

Accept a pending incoming connection on a listening socket.

Parameters:
    socket: listening socket ID

Returns:
    New socket ID for the accepted connection.

name: SNETconnect
parameters: socket As UByte, ip$, port As UInteger
doc:
Function FASTCALL SNETconnect(socket As UByte, ip$, port As UInteger) As Byte

Connect a socket to a remote host.

Parameters:
    socket: socket ID returned by SNETsocket
    ip$: IP address as a binary string, e.g. CHR$(10,0,0,1) for 10.0.0.1
    port: remote port number

name: SNETclose
doc:
Function FASTCALL SNETclose(socket As UByte) As Byte

Close a socket and release its resources.

Parameters:
    socket: socket ID to close

name: SNETrecv
parameters: socket As UByte, addr As UInteger, length As UInteger
doc:
Function FASTCALL SNETrecv(socket As UByte, addr As UInteger, length As UInteger) As Byte

Receive data from a socket into a memory buffer.

Parameters:
    socket: socket ID
    addr: destination memory address
    length: maximum bytes to receive

name: SNETsend
parameters: socket As UByte, addr As UInteger, length As UInteger
doc:
Function FASTCALL SNETsend(socket As UByte, addr As UInteger, length As UInteger) As Byte

Send data from a memory buffer through a socket.

Parameters:
    socket: socket ID
    addr: source memory address
    length: number of bytes to send

name: SNETpollfd
doc:
Function FASTCALL SNETpollfd(socket As UByte) As UByte

Poll a socket to check if data is available for reading.

Parameters:
    socket: socket ID

Returns:
    Non-zero if data is available, 0 otherwise.

name: SNETpeekUinteger
doc:
Function FASTCALL SNETpeekUinteger(addr As UInteger) As UInteger

Read a 16-bit (UInteger) value from Spectranet paged memory.

Parameters:
    addr: memory address to read from (in Spectranet paged space)

name: SNETmount
parameters: mpoint as UByte, proto$, host$, source$, userid$, passwd$
doc:
Function SNETmount(mpoint As UByte, proto$, host$, source$, userid$, passwd$) As Integer

Mount a remote filesystem on a mount point.

Parameters:
    mpoint: mount point number
    proto$: protocol name, e.g. "tnfs"
    host$: remote host name or IP
    source$: source path on the remote host
    userid$: user ID (empty string if none)
    passwd$: password (empty string if none)

Returns:
    Session number (Integer).

Example:
    SNETmount(0, "tnfs", "vexed4.alioth.net", "", "", "")

name: SNETcurrMPoint
doc:
Function SNETcurrMPoint() As Byte

Return the current active mount point number.

name: SNETsetmountpt
doc:
Sub FASTCALL SNETsetmountpt(mpoint)

Set the active mount point.

Parameters:
    mpoint: mount point number to make active

name: SNETumount
doc:
Function FASTCALL SNETumount(mpoint As UByte) As UInteger

Unmount a previously mounted filesystem.

Parameters:
    mpoint: mount point number to unmount

Example:
    SNETumount(0)

name: SNETopen
parameters: mpoint as UByte, fname$, flags as UInteger, chmod as UInteger
doc:
Function SNETopen(mpoint As UByte, fname$, flags As UInteger, chmod As UInteger) As Byte

Open a file on the remote filesystem.

Parameters:
    mpoint: mount point number
    fname$: filename
    flags: open flags (O_RDONLY, O_WRONLY, O_RDWR, O_CREAT, O_TRUNC, O_APPEND, O_EXCL)
    chmod: file permission mode (e.g. 0666o)

Returns:
    File handle (Byte), or -1 on error.

Example:
    fhandle = SNETopen(0, "myfile.bin", O_RDONLY, 0)

name: SNETfread
parameters: fhandle as UByte, addr as UInteger, size as UInteger
doc:
Function FASTCALL SNETfread(fhandle As UByte, addr As UInteger, size As UInteger) As UInteger

Read content from an open file into a memory address.

Parameters:
    fhandle: file handle returned by SNETopen or SNETfopen
    addr: destination memory address
    size: number of bytes to read

Returns:
    Number of bytes effectively read.

Example:
    SNETfread(f, 16384, 6912)  ' load a screen

name: SNETfwrite
parameters: fhandle as UByte, addr as UInteger, size as UInteger
doc:
Function FASTCALL SNETfwrite(fhandle As UByte, addr As UInteger, size As UInteger) As UInteger

Write content from a memory address into an open file.

Parameters:
    fhandle: file handle returned by SNETopen or SNETfopen
    addr: source memory address
    size: number of bytes to write

Returns:
    Number of bytes effectively written.

Example:
    SNETfwrite(f, 16384, 6912)  ' save a screen

name: SNETfopen
doc:
Function SNETfopen(mpoint As UByte, fname$, mode$) As Byte

Open a file using C-style mode string. Wraps SNETopen.

Parameters:
    mpoint: mount point number
    fname$: filename
    mode$: mode string — "r" read, "w" write/create/truncate, "a" append, "+" read+write

Returns:
    File handle (Byte), or -1 on error.

name: SNETfclose
doc:
Function FASTCALL SNETfclose(fhandle As UByte) As Byte

Close an open file handle.

Parameters:
    fhandle: file handle returned by SNETopen or SNETfopen

Returns:
    0 on success.

Example:
    SNETfclose(f)

name: SNETfseek
parameters: fhandle as UByte, op as UByte, pos as ULong
doc:
Function FASTCALL SNETfseek(fhandle As UByte, op As UByte, pos As ULong) As Byte

Change the current file read/write position.

Parameters:
    fhandle: file handle
    op: seek origin — SEEK_SET (0), SEEK_CUR (1), SEEK_END (2)
    pos: byte offset from the origin

Returns:
    0 on success.

Example:
    SNETfseek(f, SEEK_END, 0)  ' jump to end of file

name: SNETunlink
doc:
Function SNETunlink(fname$) As Byte

Delete a file from the remote filesystem.

Parameters:
    fname$: filename to delete

Returns:
    0 on success.

Example:
    SNETunlink("myfile.dat")
